detection_rules: staleness: last_tick older than stale_after_s -> YELLOW; older than dead_after_s -> RED
dominance: same binding_constraint for last N snapshots -> YELLOW (weights need rebalancing)
flatline: composite <= flatline_threshold for N consecutive snapshots -> YELLOW (Jarvis may be blind)
invalid: composite is NaN / inf / outside [0,1] -> RED

apex_predator_v0_1_31_ai_optimized_strat.bundle_name: AI-OPTIMIZED APEX PREDATOR STRATEGY STACK -- SMC/ICT playbook + RL bridge
apex_predator_v0_1_31_ai_optimized_strat.directive: Absorb the founder-brief AI-Optimized Apex Predator strategy list (6 ranked playbooks) into a pure, composable strategies package with zero pydantic/torch imports, pure functions only, and full guardrail inheritance (session gate, kill switch, regime, vol-z).
apex_predator_v0_1_31_ai_optimized_strat.theme: v0.1.30 made the product shippable; v0.1.31 makes the edge formal. Every SMC/ICT pattern (liquidity sweep, displacement, FVG, BOS, order block) is a pure function of bars. Every named strategy composes those primitives into a single StrategySignal that the policy router picks from per-asset. The regime allocator + RL wrapper slot in alongside without pulling the strategies package into torch, pydantic, or any async machinery.

1_liquidity_sweep_displacement.hypothesis: Liquidity raids create equal-level sweeps that reverse on displacement. Trade the close-back.

2_ob_breaker_retest.hypothesis: After BOS, last opposing candle is the mitigation block. Retest is the institutional re-entry zone.

3_fvg_fill_confluence.hypothesis: Unfilled FVGs on 15m/1H are imbalance reservoirs. Price returns to fill, confluence-weighted R:R.

4_mtf_trend_following.hypothesis: 200-MA aligned BOS = regime-confirmed trend. Chop regime cuts risk to zero.

5_regime_adaptive_allocation.hypothesis: Portfolio-level meta-strategy. Weight risky layers by regime + correlation + realized edge; global kill zeroes risky, preserves staking sink.

6_rl_full_automation.hypothesis: PPO/multi-agent RL end-to-end learner with a drawdown-penalizing reward. Checkpoint-gated abstention when no weights are loaded.

regime_allocator_multipliers.note: funnel.waterfall.VolRegime only defines LOW/NORMAL/HIGH; extreme-vol sizing is handled upstream by the waterfall's RiskAction path, not here.

apex_predator_v0_1_32_cross_regime_valid.directive: assist apex predator with all the task it needs to complete
apex_predator_v0_1_32_cross_regime_valid.theme: P3_PROOF.regime_validation was marked done via the firm_v3 classifier + live_sim analysis, but had no reproducible harness that isolates each regime, holds out OOS, and discriminates regime-selectivity (desirable) from sign-flip overfit (fatal). v0.1.32 ships that harness, its unit + integration tests, and its first actionable verdict.

harness.classifier_sanity_check: each RegimeSpec.axes must classify to spec.expected_label via brain.regime.classify_regime() before running the sweep -- prevents the harness silently testing the wrong regime

gate_logic.pass_condition: (>=1 regime live-tradeable) AND (no sign-flip overfit anywhere)
gate_logic.sign_flip_rule: IS expectancy_r > 0 AND OOS expectancy_r < 0 -> red flag, exclude that regime from deployment
gate_logic.why_sign_flip_only: Degradation alone flags regime-selectivity, which is expected and desirable (strategies SHOULD work better in some regimes). Only a sign flip uniquely identifies overfit -- the IS-positive edge was noise.

exit_codes.2: FAIL (no tradeable regime OR sign-flip detected)

first_run_verdict.actionable_output: deploy strategy ONLY during TRENDING regime; hard-gate HIGH_VOL (sign-flip overfit); defer RANGING and LOW_VOL until OOS trade count + expectancy improve on real data

slow_path: test_full_run_writes_artifacts_and_exits_cleanly  (subprocess-runs full script; asserts exit in {0,2} and JSON+MD artifacts)

apex_predator_v0_1_33_engine_adapter.bundle_name: ENGINE ADAPTER -- policy_router wired into the live bot path via RouterAdapter (pure bar/ctx/signal mapping + rolling buffer)
apex_predator_v0_1_33_engine_adapter.theme: v0.1.31 shipped the six strategies as pure functions; v0.1.32 shipped cross-regime OOS validation. Neither wired the stack into the real bot loop. v0.1.33 provides the adapter so any BaseBot subclass can feed a dict bar and receive a bot Signal back without either side knowing about the other.

public_api.bar_from_dict: Pure converter from bot-style dict bars (`ts/open/high/low/close/volume` or short `t/o/h/l/c/v`) to `strategies.models.Bar`. Missing OHLC raises, missing ts falls back to caller-supplied counter.
public_api.context_from_dict: Build `strategies.apex_policy.StrategyContext` from a bar dict + explicit kill_switch_active + session_allows_entries flags. Supports regime enum-with-.value or plain string, htf_bias string coercion (long/buy/up, short/sell/down), overrides last-write-wins.
public_api.strategy_signal_to_bot_signal: Map `strategies.models.StrategySignal` -> `bots.base_bot.Signal` (pydantic). Returns None for non-actionable signals. Preserves stop_distance, target, rationale_tags, strategy_meta inside `Signal.meta`.
public_api.RouterAdapter: Stateful bot-facing wrapper. Holds a rolling deque (default 300 bars), dispatches via policy_router.dispatch on each push_bar(), records last_decision for observability, exposes seed()/reset() for bootstrap.
public_api.has_eligibility_for: True if DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY has an explicit row for an asset (MNQ/NQ/BTC/ETH/SOL/XRP/PORTFOLIO). Lets callers skip adapter construction for exotic symbols.

design_guarantees.no_new_deps: uses only stdlib (collections.deque, dataclasses) + existing pydantic Signal + strategies package

design_guarantees.kill_switch_path: kill_switch_active flows through context_from_dict into StrategyContext -> _risk_mult zeros risk, regardless of detector output
design_guarantees.session_gate: session_allows_entries=False forces every strategy to FLAT via StrategyContext
design_guarantees.backwards_compatible: adapter is additive; no existing bot or test was modified

integration_contract.observability: adapter.last_decision: RouterDecision | None after each tick
integration_contract.mnq_example: adapter = RouterAdapter(asset='MNQ'); in MnqBot.on_bar, do `sig = adapter.push_bar(bar); if sig: await self.on_signal(sig)` alongside the existing 4-setup loop.

design_notes.one_trade_per_strategy: A strategy cannot stack positions on itself. Different strategies can run concurrently within the same tape.
design_notes.pessimistic_same_bar_resolution: When a bar's range touches both stop and target, STOP wins. This biases reported R downward which is the safer direction for a go/no-go calibration.
design_notes.slippage_model: 2 * slippage_bps / 10000 subtracted from raw R. Coarse but conservative; real slippage is budgeted in backtest.engine for live-replay fidelity.
design_notes.fallback_target: rl_full_automation and a handful of regime variants return signals without explicit targets. The harness constructs a 2R fallback target so those trades still produce measurable R in the report.

api_surface.Actor.STRATEGY_ROUTER: New enum value; join KILL_SWITCH / FIRM_BOARD / TRADE_ENGINE / RISK_GATE / ... in the unified log.
api_surface.RouterDecisionSink.emit: (decision, *, outcome=None, links=None) -> JournalEvent | None  -- swallows OSError
api_surface.RouterAdapter.decision_sink: Optional sink attached at construction time; push_bar emits one row per dispatch when set.

design_notes.pure_helper_first: router_decision_to_event has no I/O; it's a deterministic mapping callers can reuse for offline replays + admin audit payloads without touching a journal.
design_notes.sink_never_crashes_bot: emit() swallows OSError (disk full, permission error). Any other exception still propagates so bugs are not hidden; observability outages stay observable.
design_notes.flat_gating_default: also_log_flat=False by default -- the vast majority of ticks produce flat winners and logging them all would swamp the journal. Research runs flip the flag.
design_notes.type_checking_import: engine_adapter only references RouterDecisionSink under TYPE_CHECKING, so bots that don't wire a sink pay zero import-time cost; the obs package stays optional.

apex_predator_v0_1_39_portfolio_rebalanc.bundle_name: PORTFOLIO REBALANCER -- regime_allocator weights drive cross-layer capital transfers when the funnel drifts
apex_predator_v0_1_39_portfolio_rebalanc.theme: Close the capital-flow loop between the regime_allocator's target-weight plan and the funnel's actual per-layer equity. Waterfall moves profit; rebalancer moves position. Together they turn PORTFOLIO-tier strategy #5 into a live operational controller.

api_surface.RebalancePlan: ts_utc, total_equity_usd, sweeps (tuple of ProposedSweep), drift_pct_by_layer, target_usd_by_layer, notes, global_kill_skipped

design_notes.reuses_proposed_sweep: ProposedSweep is imported from funnel.waterfall so the orchestrator + transfer manager accept rebalance + profit sweeps through the same code path.
design_notes.strict_threshold_comparison: Drift exactly at threshold is treated as on-plan; only strictly-above drifts source transfers. Prevents oscillation around the band edge.
design_notes.greedy_pairing: Largest overweight source paired with largest underweight destination first. Minimal-transfer plan when several layers have drifted.
design_notes.kill_switch_deference: If AllocationPlan.global_kill_applied is True the rebalancer returns an empty plan with global_kill_skipped=True. Kill-switch unwind is the waterfall's job (HALT directives); two competing transfer streams during a kill would race.
design_notes.defensive_normalization: plan_rebalance scales weights if they don't sum to 1.0 -- the allocator guarantees this but hand-built AllocationPlan instances can skip the invariant.

apex_predator_v0_1_40_rebalance_executio.bundle_name: REBALANCE EXECUTION CHANNEL -- RebalancePlan sweeps route through the funnel orchestrator as real TransferRequests
apex_predator_v0_1_40_rebalance_executio.theme: Close the last gap between regime-aware target weights and live capital flow. plan_rebalance produced sweep proposals in v0.1.39; v0.1.40 wires them through the orchestrator so they become concrete inter-layer transfers governed by the same TransferManager policy + whitelist + approval gate as profit sweeps.

api_surface.rebalance_plan_to_transfers: (plan, layer_to_bot) -> list[TransferRequest]  -- pure converter. Unmapped sweeps are silently skipped; use len(result) vs len(plan.sweeps) for gap detection.
api_surface.FunnelOrchestrator.execute_rebalance: async (rebalance_plan, layer_to_bot) -> list[TransferResult]  -- routes sweeps through the existing transfer_executor injection point.
api_surface.AllocatorFn: Callable[[float], dict[str, float]]  -- replaces the prior `allocator: Any` in FunnelOrchestrator.__init__.

design_notes.separate_from_profit_sweep_channel: execute_rebalance is NOT folded into tick()/execute_tick(). Profit sweeps are driven by bot-level equity crossing a threshold; rebalance sweeps are driven by funnel-level drift from target weights. Keeping them as distinct channels means operators can disable one without touching the other.
design_notes.reuses_transfer_executor: The orchestrator's existing transfer_executor callable routes both flows. Wiring a TransferManager gives rebalance transfers the same policy + whitelist + daily-volume limit + approval gate as profit sweeps with zero additional code.
design_notes.silent_skip_on_missing_mapping: Sweeps whose source or destination LayerId is missing from layer_to_bot are dropped silently. This is an intentional escape hatch: operators can narrow the rebalance surface (e.g. freeze staking moves during a validator-unbonding window) without editing the plan.
design_notes.local_import_breaks_cycle: funnel.orchestrator imports funnel.transfer at module scope; strategies.portfolio_rebalancer also imports funnel.transfer. The rebalancer-helper import lives inside execute_rebalance to keep the static import graph acyclic without disturbing ruff TCH rules.
design_notes.lint_debt_cleanup: Pre-existing ANN401 on `allocator: Any` and TC001 on `EquityMonitor` were resolved as a drive-by; the file is now strict-ruff clean under the TCH + ANN rule sets.

apex_predator_v0_1_41_oos_qualifier.bundle_name: PER-STRATEGY OOS QUALIFIER -- walk-forward + DSR gate runs the harness through IS/OOS windows to decide keep-or-kill per strategy per asset
apex_predator_v0_1_41_oos_qualifier.theme: Compose the backtest harness with the Deflated Sharpe Ratio on a rolling walk-forward grid. Every AI-Optimized strategy now earns (or fails) its runtime allowlist slot via an explicit OOS hurdle: DSR > 0.5, avg degradation < 35%, min trades per window cleared in every window.

api_surface.PerStrategyWindow: strategy, window_id, is_n_trades, is_sharpe_like, is_total_r, is_hit_rate, oos_n_trades, oos_sharpe_like, oos_total_r, oos_hit_rate, degradation_pct, min_trades_met
api_surface.StrategyQualification: strategy, asset, n_windows, avg_is_sharpe, avg_oos_sharpe, avg_degradation_pct, dsr, n_trades_is_total, n_trades_oos_total, passes_gate, fail_reasons
api_surface.QualificationReport: asset, gate, n_windows_requested, n_windows_executed, per_window, qualifications, notes, passing_strategies, failing_strategies, as_dict()
api_surface.qualify_strategies: (bars, asset, *, gate, n_windows=4, is_fraction=0.7, ctx_builder, harness_config, eligibility, registry) -> QualificationReport

design_notes.sharpe_like_per_trade: Per-trade mean / stddev of R-multiples -- scale- invariant and matches DSR's input contract directly.
design_notes.dsr_n_trials_equals_windows: Each window is an independent backtest trial; setting n_trials to the executed-window count is the Gumbel correction Lopez de Prado prescribes. More windows deflate more aggressively.
design_notes.gate_condition_lattice: All three gate conditions must hold: DSR > threshold AND avg_degradation < max AND min_trades_per_window met in EVERY window. Explicit fail_reasons list makes the failing condition inspectable without re-running.
design_notes.non_anchored_rolling_windows: Windows are contiguous non-overlapping slices of the usable span. Avoids cross-window trade reuse so each window contributes independent OOS evidence.
design_notes.router_integration_ready: Output is already shaped for integration: report.passing_strategies intersected with DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY[asset] produces the runtime allowlist the router's `eligibility=` parameter already accepts.

phase_reconciliation.status: unchanged -- still funding-gated on P9_ROLLOUT; the qualifier gives the live policy router an objective OOS test to run before any new strategy variant earns live capital. Curve-fit edges now have to survive a Deflated Sharpe gate before they reach dispatch.
phase_reconciliation.note: v0.1.42 will plug the qualifier output into the live router's eligibility parameter as a cached intersect: passing_strategies & DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY[asset] -> runtime allowlist, refreshed on a cadence.

apex_predator_v0_1_42_runtime_allowlist_.bundle_name: RUNTIME ALLOWLIST CACHE -- OOS-qualifier verdict becomes the policy router's runtime eligibility, on a TTL cadence
apex_predator_v0_1_42_runtime_allowlist_.theme: Close the wiring gap between per-strategy OOS qualification and live dispatch. RuntimeAllowlistCache intersects report.passing_strategies with DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY[asset], caches the result with a per-entry TTL, and exposes the exact dict shape that dispatch(eligibility=...) consumes. Curve-fit strategies drop out of live dispatch the moment they fail the gate, and rejoin the moment they re-qualify -- without a human editing the eligibility table.

api_surface.AllowlistEntry: (asset, allowed, passing, base_eligible, report_asset, refreshed_at_utc)  -- frozen dataclass with as_dict()
api_surface.intersect_passing_with_base: (report, *, base_eligibility=None, now=None) -> AllowlistEntry  -- pure intersection helper
api_surface.RuntimeAllowlistCache.invalidate: (asset=None) -> None  -- clear one or all entries
api_surface.RuntimeAllowlistCache.ensure_fresh: (asset, bars, *, qualifier=None, **kwargs) -> AllowlistEntry  -- re-runs qualifier on miss/stale

design_notes.ttl_default_one_hour: Walk-forward qualification runs in seconds-to-minutes per asset; refreshing hourly keeps the live router's view fresh while leaving headroom for the ensemble without blocking the hot path.
design_notes.base_order_preserved: The intersection preserves DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY's declared order so the router's confidence-tie-breaker semantics stay stable across cache refreshes -- a passing strategy's slot in the dispatch sequence doesn't shift when an earlier peer fails qualification.
design_notes.clock_injection: clock is a Callable[[], datetime] default-factoried to datetime.now(UTC). Tests use a _ManualClock fixture to advance time deterministically -- no sleep(), no freeze-time monkey-patching.
design_notes.ensure_fresh_on_cadence: ensure_fresh() is the only path that invokes the qualifier. Call it on a cadence (hourly, session-roll, etc.) and the live router sees the freshest verdict without re-running backtests on every bar.
design_notes.as_eligibility_map_filters_stale: as_eligibility_map() silently drops stale entries. Assets that have not been re-qualified fall back to dispatch()'s hard-coded default (LSD+OB+FVG+MTF) -- never to a stale verdict. Fail-safe, not fail-closed.
design_notes.passing_preserved_even_when_not_in_base: AllowlistEntry.passing captures ALL strategies that cleared the gate, not just those also in base_eligible. This lets dashboards surface 'qualified but not yet approved' strategies for operator review.

phase_reconciliation.status: unchanged -- still funding-gated on P9_ROLLOUT; the runtime allowlist cache makes the policy router's eligibility table OOS-governed and dynamic for the first time. The six AI-Optimized strategies now earn their dispatch slot live, per tick, from the most-recent walk-forward verdict.
phase_reconciliation.note: v0.1.43 will instrument the allowlist cache with a refresh scheduler the live bot controller can tick on a cadence (e.g. on session roll or every N bars), closing the loop from bar ingest -> qualification -> live router eligibility without operator intervention.

apex_predator_v0_1_43_integrations_topol.bundle_name: INTEGRATIONS TOPOLOGY SNAPSHOT -- canonical wire map (venues, bots, funnel layers, onramps, staking, observability) plus Firm Command Center Integrations tab
apex_predator_v0_1_43_integrations_topol.theme: Everything The Firm touches was previously inferrable but not enumerated. The operator had to open bots/, venues/, funnel/, staking/, and obs/ individually to answer 'what plugs into what?'. apex_predator_v0_1_43_integrations_topol.tests_new_note: 38 integrations tests were absorbed into v0.1.42's 1959 baseline; this entry documents the artifact slice + Command Center tab wiring that shipped alongside them but was not captured in a prior bundle.

apex_predator_v0_1_43_allowlist_refresh_.bundle_name: ALLOWLIST REFRESH SCHEDULER -- bar-by-bar cadence on top of the runtime allowlist cache closes the qualification loop
apex_predator_v0_1_43_allowlist_refresh_.theme: Move the refresh cadence from operator-cron into the bot controller's per-bar hot path. The scheduler owns a RefreshTrigger policy, tracks per-asset last-refresh time + bar-count, and re-runs qualify_strategies + updates the allowlist cache automatically whenever the trigger fires. The live dispatch path now auto-loops from bar ingest to eligibility refresh with no operator involvement.

api_surface.RefreshTrigger: (every_n_bars=None, every_seconds=None, min_bars_before_first=50)  -- frozen dataclass; at least one trigger must be set
api_surface.AllowlistScheduler: (cache, trigger, clock=datetime.now(UTC))  -- ticks the cache on a per-bar cadence
api_surface.AllowlistScheduler.tick: (asset, bars, *, qualifier=None, **qualifier_kwargs) -> AllowlistEntry | None  -- returns entry if refreshed
api_surface.AllowlistScheduler.force_refresh: (asset, bars, ...)  -- bypass trigger; always refreshes + updates bookkeeping

design_notes.cheap_no_op_tick: tick() does O(1) work when the trigger has not fired -- two dict lookups, one subtraction. Safe to call on every bar.
design_notes.bar_or_time_whichever_first: When both triggers are set, tick() fires on the first one to clear. This lets operators set 'every 500 bars OR every 10 minutes' and have the qualifier refresh snappily in high-throughput tapes while still refreshing on slow tapes.
design_notes.warmup_blocks_first_only: min_bars_before_first gates the FIRST refresh, not subsequent ones. Once the cold-start window has accrued enough bars, follow-up refreshes fire on the bar-count / time trigger alone.
design_notes.reset_clears_bookkeeping_not_cache: reset() wipes the scheduler's last-refresh memory but leaves the cache untouched. That way a session roll can re-start the cadence while still using the prior session's allowlist until the first re-refresh.
design_notes.kwargs_opaque_forward: tick() accepts **qualifier_kwargs and forwards them verbatim. Operators can pass gate=, n_windows=, harness_config=, registry= without the scheduler knowing what they mean.
design_notes.force_refresh_for_events: force_refresh() bypasses every trigger and still updates bookkeeping -- correct semantics for operator-driven refreshes on configuration change or an external 'kill + re-qualify' command.

phase_reconciliation.status: unchanged -- still funding-gated on P9_ROLLOUT; the refresh scheduler makes the OOS-governance loop fully self-driving: bar ingest -> qualifier -> cache -> dispatch, ticked on every bar, zero operator in the loop.
phase_reconciliation.note: v0.1.44 will plug the scheduler into the live bot controller's on_bar path so the dispatch() call reads cache.as_eligibility_map() on every bar and the per-asset allowlist drives real entries / exits.

apex_predator_v0_1_44_live_router_adapte.bundle_name: LIVE ROUTER ADAPTER WIRING -- RouterAdapter.push_bar ticks the allowlist scheduler before dispatch; OOS qualification now governs the live bot trading path
apex_predator_v0_1_44_live_router_adapte.theme: Close the last wire between OOS qualification and live dispatch. RouterAdapter now accepts an optional AllowlistScheduler which it ticks on every push_bar; the scheduler's cache map merges with the static eligibility override (static wins) to form the eligibility argument the router actually sees. Scheduler failures are wrapped in try/except so the hot trading loop never dies on a qualifier bug.

strategies: strategies/engine_adapter.py (+allowlist_scheduler, +scheduler_kwargs, +_tick_scheduler_safely, +_effective_eligibility, push_bar merges scheduler into dispatch)

api_surface.RouterAdapter.allowlist_scheduler: AllowlistScheduler | None  -- new optional field. When set, push_bar ticks it before dispatch.
api_surface.RouterAdapter.scheduler_kwargs: Mapping[str, object] | None  -- forwarded verbatim to scheduler.tick on every bar.
api_surface.RouterAdapter._tick_scheduler_safely: () -> None  -- private; wraps scheduler.tick in a blanket try/except so qualifier failure never crashes push_bar.
api_surface.RouterAdapter._effective_eligibility: () -> dict[str, tuple[StrategyId, ...]] | None  -- merges scheduler cache with static override; static wins on per-asset conflict.

design_notes.tick_before_dispatch: The scheduler tick happens BEFORE the dispatch call in push_bar, not after. That way a refresh that fires on THIS bar's tick already governs THIS bar's eligibility -- the freshest possible verdict.
design_notes.static_wins_on_conflict: When both the static eligibility dict and the scheduler's cache map have an entry for the same asset, the static dict wins. Rationale: static eligibility is an explicit operator choice and must not be overridden by automated qualification.
design_notes.blanket_try_except_on_tick: Scheduler failures (e.g. qualifier bugs, cache errors) are swallowed at the adapter boundary. Letting a qualifier exception propagate would mean one strategy's bug takes the bot offline. Instead the cache stays at its last-known-good state and dispatch falls back.
design_notes.fallback_chain: Effective eligibility precedence: static override -> scheduler cache -> None (which dispatch() treats as DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY). Fail-safe: the bot never runs in a state with no eligible strategies unless the operator explicitly configured that.
design_notes.zero_overhead_when_scheduler_absent: When allowlist_scheduler is None (the default) the new code paths short-circuit immediately and _effective_eligibility returns self.eligibility unchanged. All 78 pre-existing engine-adapter tests still pass with no modifications.

phase_reconciliation.status: unchanged -- still funding-gated on P9_ROLLOUT; the live router adapter is now fully governed by the OOS qualification loop. The six AI-Optimized strategies earn their dispatch slot on the live bot by walk-forward + DSR evidence; no hand-curated eligibility table in the hot path.

apex_predator_v0_1_45_live_bot_controlle.bundle_name: LIVE BOT CONTROLLER WIRING -- MnqBot and EthPerpBot auto-wire the full AI-Optimized stack (cache + scheduler + adapter) at start-up via build_live_adapter()
apex_predator_v0_1_45_live_bot_controlle.theme: Give live bots a one-line switch to self-construct the full OOS-governed dispatch pipeline. The factory module exposes canonical defaults (TTL=7200s, refresh=288 bars OR 3600s, warmup=200 bars). Bots get two new kwargs: auto_wire_ai_strategies (toggle) and ai_strategy_config (override). When the toggle is on and no operator-supplied adapter exists, start() builds and installs one.

bots: bots/eth_perp/bot.py (same wiring; strips USDT suffix when deriving the strategy asset key)

api_surface.build_live_adapter: (asset, *, buffer_bars=300, ttl_seconds=7200.0, refresh_every_n_bars=288, refresh_every_seconds=3600.0, min_bars_before_first=200, base_eligibility=None, eligibility_override=None, decision_sink=None, scheduler_kwargs=None, clock=None, kill_switch_active=False, session_allows_entries=True) -> RouterAdapter
api_surface.MnqBot.auto_wire_ai_strategies: bool = False  -- when True + no strategy_adapter, start() builds one via build_live_adapter()
api_surface.MnqBot.ai_strategy_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None  -- forwarded as **kwargs to build_live_adapter on auto-wire
api_surface.EthPerpBot.auto_wire_ai_strategies: same contract; strips USDT suffix from symbol

design_notes.defaults_sized_to_qualifier: TTL=7200s is 2x the 3600s wall-clock trigger so the cache is always fresh between scheduler ticks. 288 bars = 24h of 5m data. 200-bar warmup matches the DSR estimator's minimum sample size.
design_notes.bar_or_time_whichever_first: refresh_every_n_bars and refresh_every_seconds both fire on whichever-first semantics. Fast tapes refresh every hour (the time trigger wins); slow tapes refresh every 288 bars (the bar-count trigger wins).
design_notes.static_override_still_wins: eligibility_override is passed to the RouterAdapter's static eligibility field, not the cache. When both are populated, the RouterAdapter's _effective_eligibility merges them with static-wins semantics.
design_notes.clock_shared_across_cache_and_scheduler: When `clock=` is injected, BOTH the cache and the scheduler receive the same callable. Their notions of 'now' stay aligned so TTL expiry and trigger bookkeeping don't drift under accelerated test clocks.
design_notes.auto_wire_is_start_time_not_init_time: Auto-wire runs in async start(), not __init__. Rationale: __init__ must stay I/O-free (unit tests construct bots under pytest collection). start() is the lifecycle event the operator explicitly calls at bot launch, so that's where heavy construction belongs.
design_notes.local_import_at_start_time: build_live_adapter is imported inside start() rather than at module top. Keeps the bot importable in environments that do not load the strategies subpackage (e.g. the legacy 4-setup test suite).
design_notes.eth_symbol_suffix_strip: EthPerpBot's config.symbol is ETHUSDT (Bybit format) but the strategy layer keys off the bare asset ETH. Start() strips the USDT suffix before handing the asset to the factory; this keeps the venue layer and strategy layer independent.

phase_reconciliation.status: unchanged -- still funding-gated on P9_ROLLOUT; the OOS-governed trading loop is now end-to-end self-driving from bar ingest through to the live bot's order-router boundary. No operator hand-wiring remains.
phase_reconciliation.note: v0.1.46 shifts from pipeline-wiring to risk-model evolution: the ADAPTIVE SIZING ENGINE. Position size becomes a function of (regime x confluence x historical-success x equity-band) with tiered multipliers (CONVICTION, STANDARD, REDUCED, PROBE, SKIP). High-confluence trend setups earn 2-3x sizing; low-confluence ranging setups get probe-sized or skipped entirely. The engine self-evolves on live trade outcomes and emits a self-review signal when drawdown breaches a regime-specific band.

apex_predator_v0_1_46_adaptive_sizing_en.bundle_name: ADAPTIVE SIZING ENGINE -- position size is a function of (regime x confluence x htf_bias x equity_band x prior_success) mapped to tiered multipliers (CONVICTION 3.0x/2.0x, STANDARD 1.0x, REDUCED 0.5x, PROBE 0.25x, SKIP 0.0x).
apex_predator_v0_1_46_adaptive_sizing_en.theme: Install the Apex thesis: the best 5-10% of setups deserve 2-3x the capital, the worst 30% deserve probe sizing or a skip. Convert size from a static risk_usd/stop_distance formula into an axis-weighted, regime-aware, self-evolving decision with a full rationale trail. When odds are stacked, take the sniper shot. When stats say 'you're fooling yourself,' downsize or sit out.
apex_predator_v0_1_46_adaptive_sizing_en.operator_directive_quote: anything rated super high confidence of success needs to be sized 2-3 times bigger than normal, anything rated low chance or too much uncertainty not enough confluence gets sized down ... if you know you know and you take your shot ... sometimes you have to roll the dice when the odds are stacked in your favor and you take out the sniper and shoot ... make it self evolve with live data as time goes on, make this regime based as well, as with time the only thing guaranteed is change, it has to be adaptable and learn to run from its first steps to become a marathon runner.

api_surface.SizeTier: StrEnum -- CONVICTION / STANDARD / REDUCED / PROBE / SKIP
api_surface.PriorSuccessMetrics: frozen dataclass -- n_trades, hit_rate, expectancy_r, avg_win_r, avg_loss_r, consecutive_losses, consecutive_wins. This is the feedback hook the engine uses to self-evolve on live trade outcomes.
api_surface.SizingContext: frozen dataclass -- asset, strategy, side, regime, confluence_score, htf_bias, equity_band, prior, base_risk_pct=1.0, kill_switch_active=False, session_allows_entries=True. All sizer inputs live on one immutable value object.
api_surface.SizingPolicy: frozen dataclass -- axis weights + tier thresholds + multipliers + safety bounds. Tunable without code changes. Defaults: weight_regime=0.25, weight_confluence=0.30, weight_htf=0.10, weight_equity=0.15, weight_prior=0.20.
api_surface.SizingVerdict: frozen dataclass -- tier, multiplier, adjusted_risk_pct, total_score, axis_scores (dict), rationale (tuple[str, ...]). Every decision is explainable and auditable.
api_surface.compute_size: (ctx: SizingContext, policy: SizingPolicy = DEFAULT_SIZING_POLICY) -> SizingVerdict -- pure function. Hard gates short-circuit to SKIP; otherwise weighted sum of axis scores -> tier mapping -> risk clamp.
api_surface.classify_equity_band: (equity, high_water, *, growth_threshold=1.02, drawdown_threshold=0.95, critical_threshold=0.90) -> EquityBand -- raises ValueError for non-positive high_water.
api_surface.score_regime: (regime, strategy) -> float in [-0.5, +1.0] -- rewards regime-strategy fit (e.g. TRENDING + MTF_TREND_FOLLOWING = +1.0; RANGING + trend strategy = -0.5).
api_surface.score_confluence: (confluence_score) -> float in [-1.0, +1.0] -- maps confluence_score linearly from the observed domain into [-1, +1].
api_surface.score_htf_agreement: (side, htf_bias) -> float in {-1.0, 0.0, +0.5} -- punishes counter-HTF trades; rewards alignment; neutral when htf_bias is None.
api_surface.score_equity_band: (band) -> float in {+0.5, 0.0, -0.5, -1.0} -- GROWTH bonus, NEUTRAL neutral, DRAWDOWN caution, CRITICAL heavy penalty.
api_surface.score_prior_success: (prior) -> float in [-1.0, +1.0] -- blends hit_rate+expectancy+streak into a single normalized score; demands min n_trades to escape neutral.

design_notes.axis_weights_sum_to_1: regime=0.25, confluence=0.30, htf=0.10, equity=0.15, prior=0.20. Total=1.00. Confluence is the single heaviest axis because it already aggregates multiple signals under the hood; prior_success is the second heaviest because it's the self-evolving feedback signal.
design_notes.tier_thresholds_as_pct_of_ceiling: Positive score ceiling is about +0.875. CONVICTION 3.0x at >=0.65 ~= 74% of ceiling (true sniper shots only). CONVICTION 2.0x at >=0.45 ~= 51% of ceiling (high-conviction but not textbook). STANDARD at >=0.15 (the baseline go-live floor). REDUCED at >=-0.10. PROBE at >=-0.35. Below that is SKIP.
design_notes.hard_overrides_short_circuit: kill_switch_active and session_allows_entries=False produce SKIP BEFORE any axis computation. The verdict rationale explicitly names the override so the observer can tell a kill-switched skip from a score-based skip.
design_notes.safety_bounds_clamp_risk: Non-probe/non-skip tiers clamp adjusted_risk_pct to [min_risk_pct=0.10, max_risk_pct=5.00]. PROBE stays small by construction (floor 0.01%). SKIP is exactly 0. This prevents a pathological policy tune or a bad axis score from ever exceeding portfolio risk limits.
design_notes.self_evolving_feedback: PriorSuccessMetrics is updated by the trade-outcome layer (not this module). A losing streak of 3 with negative expectancy pulls prior_success axis deep negative -> next setup same strategy -> REDUCED/PROBE. A run of wins with positive expectancy upsizes. No manual knob required.
design_notes.equity_band_monotonic_with_drawdown: classify_equity_band maps equity/high_water to GROWTH (>=1.02), NEUTRAL (>=0.95), DRAWDOWN (>=0.90), CRITICAL (<0.90). Monotonic: as drawdown deepens the equity-band axis score decreases; combined with the weighted-sum gate, CRITICAL band + any other axis mildly negative flips STANDARD -> REDUCED. The user's 'some periods we completely downsize due to too much inconsistent uncertainty' path.
design_notes.pure_function_zero_mutation: compute_size takes a frozen SizingContext and returns a frozen SizingVerdict. No mutable state inside the sizer itself. All feedback arrives via the PriorSuccessMetrics input. Trivially thread-safe; trivially replayable; trivially auditable.
design_notes.rationale_trail_every_decision: SizingVerdict.rationale is a tuple of strings naming the dominant factors behind the tier call ('regime trending confirms MTF_TREND_FOLLOWING', 'confluence above sniper threshold', 'equity in drawdown band caps size'). Every decision is auditable without replaying the math.

CONVICTION_3x.rationale: Sniper shot. Regime+confluence+htf+prior all aligned. Equity band at least NEUTRAL. No counter-HTF. This is the 'odds stacked in your favor, take the shot' path.

CONVICTION_2x.rationale: High conviction but not textbook. Typically one axis is merely neutral rather than positive, but the dominant axes are strong.

STANDARD_1x.rationale: Baseline tradeable setup. Meets gate but doesn't earn size bonus.

REDUCED_0_5x.rationale: Mildly negative net score. Trade-able but with reduced risk. Typical in DRAWDOWN band or on a short losing streak.

PROBE_0_25x.rationale: Uncertain conditions, but still worth a probe to feel the market. Floor 0.01% risk.

SKIP_0x.rationale: Too much inconsistency / counter-HTF / critical drawdown / kill_switch_active. Sit out and question the strategy.

notable_cases: sniper_shot_path: regime TRENDING + confluence high + htf aligned + prior winning + GROWTH band -> CONVICTION 3.0x
risk_off_path: kill_switch_active -> SKIP regardless of axis scores
critical_drawdown_suppresses_conviction: CRITICAL band can veto a high-confluence trade into REDUCED/PROBE
losing_streak_downsizes: 3 consecutive_losses + negative expectancy -> REDUCED/PROBE on next setup
safety_bounds_clamp: max_risk_pct=5.00 caps conviction trades even if the policy multiplier tries to push higher

phase_reconciliation.status: unchanged -- still funding-gated on P9_ROLLOUT. The trading loop now has a pure-function, axis-weighted, regime-aware sizing engine ready to be wired into the live bots' _size_from_signal path. The engine itself is self-evolving via PriorSuccessMetrics feedback.
phase_reconciliation.note: v0.1.47 will ship the PERFORMANCE RETROSPECTIVE ENGINE: on drawdown breach, regime shift, or a losing streak threshold, the engine emits a structured self-review ('what stopped working, what to adjust, deviation path') that feeds back into PriorSuccessMetrics and logs a regime-conditional playbook note. This closes the 'what should I have done differently? how should I deviate to return or exceed previous baseline?' loop from the operator's directive. v0.1.48+ wires compute_size() into MnqBot/EthPerpBot's _size_from_signal so tier multipliers actually drive live position size.

apex_predator_v0_1_47_performance_retros.bundle_name: PERFORMANCE RETROSPECTIVE ENGINE -- on drawdown breach, regime shift, losing streak, or expectancy decay, emit a structured three-layer self-review (WHAT_STOPPED_WORKING / WHAT_TO_ADJUST / DEVIATION_PATH) plus concrete SizingPolicy deltas.
apex_predator_v0_1_47_performance_retros.theme: Close the 'what stopped working / what to adjust / how to deviate back to baseline' loop from the operator's directive. v0.1.46 installed the adaptive sizer; v0.1.47 installs the self-questioning layer on top. Pure-function engine, feeds deltas back into SizingPolicy so the next bar's sizer is tighter on the axes that just failed.
apex_predator_v0_1_47_performance_retros.operator_directive_quote: some periods we completely downsize due to too much inconsistent uncertainty and at that point we question our optimizations and judgments, what's wrong? what can be improved? what stopped working? what should i have done differently? how should i deviate to return or exceed previous baseline?

api_surface.RetrospectiveTrigger: StrEnum -- DRAWDOWN_BREACH / REGIME_SHIFT / LOSING_STREAK / EXPECTANCY_DECAY / SCHEDULED_REVIEW. The trigger is supplied by the live bot based on its own monitoring.
api_surface.FindingCategory: StrEnum -- WHAT_STOPPED_WORKING (descriptive) / WHAT_TO_ADJUST (prescriptive) / DEVIATION_PATH (recovery plan). Every report has findings across all three categories.
api_surface.FindingSeverity: StrEnum -- INFO / WARN / CRITICAL. Scaled to the magnitude of the measured gap vs baseline.
api_surface.StrategyPerformanceSnapshot: frozen dataclass -- strategy, regime, window, n_trades, hit_rate, expectancy_r, max_drawdown_r, consecutive_losses/wins. Built by the trade journal. .is_meaningful gates n_trades >= 5.
api_surface.RetrospectiveContext: frozen dataclass -- the engine's full input: asset, strategy, trigger, timestamp, current + baseline snapshots, prior/current regime, prior/current equity band, high_water_equity, current_equity, optional recent_verdicts tuple.
api_surface.RetrospectiveFinding: frozen dataclass -- category, headline, detail, severity, confidence (0..1), axis_key. axis_key ties the finding back to one of the v0.1.46 sizer axes (regime/confluence/htf/equity/prior) for dashboards.
api_surface.PolicyDelta: frozen dataclass -- field_name (on SizingPolicy), old_value, new_value, rationale. Apply a tuple of deltas via apply_policy_deltas() to build a revised SizingPolicy.
api_surface.RetrospectiveReport: frozen dataclass -- orchestrator output. findings, recommended_policy_deltas, overall_severity (0..1), expectancy_drop_r, hit_rate_drop, max_dd_delta_r, regime_changed, equity_band_changed.
api_surface.RetrospectivePolicy: frozen dataclass -- trigger thresholds + severity weights + delta magnitudes. Defaults: drawdown_breach_ratio=0.95, critical_breach_ratio=0.90, losing_streak_threshold=3, expectancy_decay_r=0.3, hit_rate_decay=0.15, min_trades_for_judgment=5. Severity weights: 0.40/0.25/0.25/0.10 for expectancy/hit_rate/drawdown/streak.
api_surface.detect_drawdown_breach: (equity, high_water, *, drawdown_threshold=0.95) -> bool. Raises ValueError on high_water <= 0.
api_surface.detect_regime_shift: (prior: RegimeLabel | None, current: RegimeLabel) -> bool. None prior returns False (no shift on first tick).
api_surface.detect_losing_streak: (consecutive_losses, *, threshold=3) -> bool. Negative inputs treated as zero.
api_surface.detect_expectancy_decay: (current_expectancy_r, baseline_expectancy_r, *, decay_threshold=0.3) -> bool. Only fires if baseline was positive -- a losing baseline cannot 'decay' in this sense.
api_surface.compute_severity: (ctx, policy) -> float 0..1. Weighted blend of expectancy drop, hit-rate drop, drawdown delta, and losing streak. Saturates at each weight's ceiling; total saturates at 1.0; never negative.
api_surface.diagnose: (ctx, policy) -> tuple[RetrospectiveFinding, ...] across WHAT_STOPPED_WORKING, WHAT_TO_ADJUST, DEVIATION_PATH. Always non-empty (clean window yields an INFO marker).
api_surface.propose_policy_deltas: (ctx, current_sizing_policy=DEFAULT_SIZING_POLICY, retro_policy=DEFAULT_RETROSPECTIVE_POLICY) -> tuple[PolicyDelta, ...]. Each delta names a real SizingPolicy field, carries its rationale.
api_surface.build_retrospective: (ctx, current_sizing_policy, policy) -> RetrospectiveReport. The orchestrator. Runs diagnose + propose_policy_deltas + compute_severity and packages into one report.
api_surface.apply_policy_deltas: (policy: SizingPolicy, deltas: tuple[PolicyDelta, ...]) -> SizingPolicy. Uses dataclasses.replace. Raises AttributeError on unknown field names (catches delta-catalog drift).

design_notes.pure_functions_only: No I/O, no threads, no mutable module state. The retrospective is a pure function from frozen inputs to a frozen report. Trivially replayable; trivially auditable; trivially thread-safe.
design_notes.triggers_outside_engine: The live bot owns monitoring and decides when to call. The engine provides detectors as composable predicates but does not fire itself. This keeps the engine stateless.
design_notes.deltas_are_recommendations: The engine proposes SizingPolicy changes; the operator or orchestrator decides whether to apply. This preserves 'we question our optimizations' -- the engine does not silently rewrite its own policy.
design_notes.three_layer_diagnosis: WHAT_STOPPED_WORKING is descriptive (what happened). WHAT_TO_ADJUST is prescriptive (concrete tweaks). DEVIATION_PATH is the recovery plan (what to do until metrics recover). Each layer carries severity and confidence.
design_notes.severity_composition: 0.40 expectancy drop (saturate 1R) + 0.25 hit-rate drop (saturate 25 pts) + 0.25 drawdown delta (saturate 3R) + 0.10 losing streak (saturate 5 trades). Each driver at its weight ceiling; total at 1.0; never negative.
design_notes.delta_catalog: Six SizingPolicy fields can be recommended: conviction_high_threshold, conviction_low_threshold, standard_threshold, max_risk_pct, min_risk_pct, weight_prior. Each delta carries its own rationale so the audit trail is self-explaining.
design_notes.wire_compatible_with_v0_1_46: Uses the same RegimeLabel, EquityBand, SizingPolicy, SizingVerdict. Deltas apply via dataclasses.replace. One import line, one function call to re-evaluate the next trade.
design_notes.healthy_window_yields_info_marker: A clean window (no degradation vs baseline) emits a single INFO-severity finding rather than an empty findings tuple. Downstream consumers always see a report shape they can render.
design_notes.none_prior_treated_as_unchanged: detect_regime_shift(None, X) -> False. report.equity_band_changed when prior_band is None -> False. This handles first-tick / fresh-start cases without a special boolean.

trigger_semantics.DRAWDOWN_BREACH: equity/high_water crossed below the configured ratio (0.95 default; 0.90 is CRITICAL).
trigger_semantics.REGIME_SHIFT: prior_regime is not current_regime. The live bot's regime classifier owns the prior/current state transitions.
trigger_semantics.LOSING_STREAK: consecutive_losses >= threshold (3 default). Counter is owned by the trade-outcome layer.
trigger_semantics.EXPECTANCY_DECAY: baseline - current >= decay_threshold (0.3 R default). Only fires if baseline was positive.
trigger_semantics.SCHEDULED_REVIEW: Operator-triggered periodic review with no specific alarm -- useful for daily/weekly introspection reports.

notable_cases: test_applied_deltas_produce_tighter_policy: end-to-end: expectancy drop + hit-rate drop + losing streak + drawdown band -> 5 deltas -> new SizingPolicy has tighter thresholds, lower max_risk, higher weight_prior.
test_applied_policy_changes_sizer_behavior: same marginal setup sized under default vs tightened policy -> sizer tier is never upgraded by the retrospective (only downgraded or equal).
test_critical_band_locks_out_conviction: CRITICAL band -> max_risk_pct reduced to 30% of original. Even a CONVICTION 3x multiplier gets clamped.
test_severity_clamped_to_one: all four drivers saturate -> severity = 1.0 exactly (0.40 + 0.25 + 0.25 + 0.10).
test_raises_on_unknown_field: PolicyDelta with non-existent SizingPolicy field -> AttributeError. Catches delta-catalog drift.

The system can size up on conviction, size down on uncertainty, AND question its own policy when the expected edge stops materializing.

apex_predator_v0_1_48_live_bot_retrospec.bundle_name: LIVE BOT RETROSPECTIVE WIRING -- RetrospectiveManager encapsulates the rolling journal, trigger detection, sink dispatch, and cooldown-guarded SizingPolicy mutation around v0.1.47's pure-function engine. Two methods (record_trade + on_bar) are all a bot needs to run a self-evolving policy.
apex_predator_v0_1_48_live_bot_retrospec.theme: v0.1.47 shipped the pure engine. v0.1.48 encapsulates the boilerplate every caller would otherwise hand-roll: rolling window, streak counters, high-water tracking, trigger priority, cooldown guard, sink isolation. Turns the feedback loop from a pure function into a live, self-evolving policy manager that any bot can wire in with two method calls.
apex_predator_v0_1_48_live_bot_retrospec.operator_directive_quote: how should i deviate to return or exceed previous baseline? -- now an executable loop, not a design note.

api_surface.TradeOutcome: frozen dataclass -- timestamp_utc, strategy, regime, pnl_r, equity_after. One closed trade; fed to RetrospectiveManager.record_trade.
api_surface.RetrospectiveManager: class -- stateful adapter around the v0.1.47 engine. __init__ takes starting_equity, initial_policy, retrospective_policy, window_size, baseline_window_size, cooldown_seconds, clock, report_sink, apply_deltas. Read-only properties: current_policy, high_water_equity, current_equity, equity_band, reports_emitted, deltas_applied. Public methods: record_trade, on_bar, force_retrospective, reset, stats.
api_surface.record_trade: (outcome: TradeOutcome) -> RetrospectiveReport | None. Updates journal + streak counters + equity + high-water; picks highest-priority trigger; builds report; pushes to sink; applies deltas under cooldown guard.
api_surface.on_bar: (*, regime: RegimeLabel, equity: float | None = None) -> RetrospectiveReport | None. Updates regime state and optional equity; fires only on regime-shift detection. Picks the most-traded bucket for the new regime (or overall) to build the context from.
api_surface.force_retrospective: (*, strategy: StrategyId, regime: RegimeLabel, trigger: RetrospectiveTrigger = SCHEDULED_REVIEW) -> RetrospectiveReport | None. Operator-triggered review; bypasses trigger detectors. Honors cooldown for policy mutation. Returns None if the target bucket is empty.
api_surface.reset: () -> None. Forgets all journal state and restores starting equity / high-water. Keeps current_policy -- operator decision whether to restore defaults belongs at a higher layer.
api_surface.stats: () -> dict[str, Any]. Dashboard/diagnostic snapshot: current_equity, high_water_equity, equity_band, reports_emitted, deltas_applied, last_regime_seen, last_trigger_at, per-bucket trade + streak counters.

design_notes.stateful_adapter_stateless_engine: The engine is unchanged. Manager owns state: rolling journal, streak counters, high-water, prior regime, last-trigger timestamp. Engine stays replayable/auditable; manager stays honest about persistence.
design_notes.trigger_priority: DRAWDOWN_BREACH > LOSING_STREAK > EXPECTANCY_DECAY. One report per trade close, not a queue of overlapping reports. The live bot sees the most urgent signal first.
design_notes.cooldown_guarded_mutation: apply_deltas=True rewrites current_policy at most once per cooldown_seconds. Prevents oscillating regimes from flipping the sizer on every bar. Reports still fire every trigger -- only the policy mutation is gated.
design_notes.bucketed_by_strategy_regime: Each (StrategyId, RegimeLabel) has its own journal + streak counters. A strategy that shines in TREND_UP but drags in RANGE is not averaged across the two states. Regime-shift triggers pick the most-traded bucket for the new regime; fallback is the most-traded bucket overall if the new regime is empty.
design_notes.bounded_journal: baseline_window_size is the hard cap per bucket; older trades are dropped from the left. window_size is the 'current' snapshot taken from the right tail. baseline = everything before that tail. Validated at init: baseline_window_size > window_size, starting_equity > 0.
design_notes.fails_safe: Sink raising -> log + continue. Engine raising -> log + return None. PolicyDelta targeting a nonexistent SizingPolicy field -> log AttributeError + skip that mutation. The live bot never crashes from a retrospective.
design_notes.injected_clock: All timestamp-dependent behavior reads from the injected clock callable. Tests drive the clock forward deterministically; production uses a default of datetime.now(UTC).
design_notes.equity_band_transition_capture: Manager tracks last_equity_band across report emissions, so the report's equity_band_changed flag reflects the real transition rather than None -> current on every tick.

notable_cases: test_full_scenario_drawdown_and_recovery: end-to-end smoke. Win establishes high-water -> loss drops equity to 94% triggering both DRAWDOWN_BREACH and DRAWDOWN-band delta -> policy rewritten with tighter max_risk_pct -> cooldown blocks second mutation on immediate follow-up loss -> past cooldown, winning trade above breach ratio does not mutate again.
test_cooldown_prevents_immediate_second_mutation: two triggers within the cooldown window emit two reports but mutate the policy exactly once.
test_sink_failure_isolated: sink raises on every call -> reports still counted, deltas still applied, manager never propagates the exception.
test_trigger_priority_drawdown_beats_streak: simultaneous drawdown breach AND losing streak -> report.trigger is DRAWDOWN_BREACH.
test_regime_shift_picks_most_traded_bucket: multiple buckets in new regime -> the one with more trades wins the retrospective.

api_surface.MnqBot_init_kwargs: retrospective_manager: RetrospectiveManager | None = None; auto_wire_retrospective: bool = False; retrospective_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None; default_retrospective_strategy: StrategyId | None = None. All four default to the pre-v0.1.49 behavior when unset.
api_surface.MnqBot_retrospective_manager: read-only property returning the wired manager or None.
api_surface.MnqBot_record_trade_outcome: (*, pnl_r: float, strategy: StrategyId | None = None, regime: RegimeLabel | None = None) -> RetrospectiveReport | None. Feeds a closed trade into the manager. Strategy defaults to the per-symbol fallback; regime defaults to RegimeLabel.TRANSITION. Failure-isolated.
api_surface.EthPerpBot_init_kwargs: Same four kwargs as MnqBot; interface is uniform across the fleet.
api_surface.EthPerpBot_infer_regime: @staticmethod (bar) -> RegimeType. ETH-calibrated ADX thresholds (25 / 18 vs MNQ 30 / 20) so TRENDING is recognized at the lower ADX values typical of crypto.
api_surface.EthPerpBot_record_trade_outcome: Same signature as MnqBot; shares the adapter.
api_surface.bots.retrospective_adapter.map_regime: (RegimeType | None) -> RegimeLabel. None -> RegimeLabel.TRANSITION. TRENDING -> TRENDING (value='TREND'); TRANSITION -> TRANSITION; RANGING -> RANGING (value='RANGE').
api_surface.bots.retrospective_adapter.build_trade_o: (*, strategy, regime, pnl_r, equity_after, timestamp_utc=None) -> TradeOutcome. timestamp_utc defaults to datetime.now(UTC).
api_surface.bots.retrospective_adapter.default_strat: (symbol: str) -> StrategyId. Looks up DEFAULT_STRATEGY_FOR_BOT; unknown symbols fall back to StrategyId.MTF_TREND_FOLLOWING.
api_surface.bots.retrospective_adapter.DEFAULT_STRAT: dict[str, StrategyId] mapping fleet symbols (MNQ, NQ, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT, XRPUSDT) to their default StrategyId. Currently all MTF_TREND_FOLLOWING; operators can override per-bot via default_retrospective_strategy kwarg.

design_notes.opt_in_only: No BaseBot field changes. A bot constructed without any retrospective kwargs behaves exactly as before. 55 pre-existing bot tests pass unchanged.
design_notes.lazy_imports: bots/mnq/bot.py and bots/eth_perp/bot.py use TYPE_CHECKING for manager types and import the adapter + engine modules inside methods so bots.base_bot does not pull the strategies subpackage into its import graph.
design_notes.explicit_regime_mapping: The adapter uses a dict from RegimeType to RegimeLabel rather than string identity. RegimeLabel.HIGH_VOL is available to callers that infer it elsewhere (e.g., ATR-based volatility regime classifiers) but the baseline MNQ / ETH ADX classifiers do not emit it.
design_notes.per_bot_default_strategy: record_trade_outcome without a strategy kwarg picks from DEFAULT_STRATEGY_FOR_BOT keyed on the bot's config.symbol. Legacy 4-setup MNQ logic doesn't produce StrategyId values; the per-symbol default keeps the bucket key stable.
design_notes.failure_isolation: Both on_bar tick and record_trade_outcome wrap manager calls in try/except + logger.warning. If the manager raises, the bot logs and continues. BoomManager stub tests assert this on both paths.
design_notes.eth_regime_thresholds_are_separate: EthPerpBot does not inherit MnqBot.regime_filter. Crypto perps need a lower ADX bar for 'trending' (25 / 18) than index futures (30 / 20). Each bot classifies its own market honestly.
design_notes.no_position_close_auto_hook: v0.1.49 exposes record_trade_outcome but does NOT auto-invoke it from update_state(fill). The fill pipeline does not yet carry risk_at_entry needed to compute pnl_r. v0.1.50 will wire the entry-fill risk capture and the close-fill trigger so the record_trade call is automatic.

notable_cases: test_auto_wire_at_start: MnqBot with auto_wire_retrospective=True auto-instantiates a manager seeded from starting_capital_usd.
test_auto_wire_respects_existing_manager: an operator-supplied manager is never overwritten by auto-wire.
test_trending_bar_pushes_trend_label: on_bar tick pushes the mapped RegimeLabel.TRENDING into the manager; stats() reflects it.
test_losing_streak_eventually_fires_report: three consecutive losses trigger a LOSING_STREAK retrospective end-to-end.
test_on_bar_tick_survives_manager_exception: BoomManager raising inside on_bar does not crash the bot's on_bar call.
test_same_manager_receives_from_both_bots: one shared manager receives TradeOutcomes from both MnqBot and EthPerpBot; buckets are separate by strategy + regime.
test_trending_threshold_is_lower_than_mnq: ADX 25 is TRENDING in EthPerpBot but TRANSITION in MnqBot.

phase_reconciliation.note: v0.1.50 will auto-invoke record_trade_outcome from the fill pipeline: entry fill stashes risk_at_entry on the position; close fill computes pnl_r = realized_pnl / risk_at_entry and calls record_trade_outcome. At that point the live trading loop runs the feedback loop with zero manual intervention.

apex_predator_v0_1_50_fill_pipeline_auto.bundle_name: FILL-PIPELINE AUTO-INVOCATION -- stash entry risk on signal fire, auto-invoke record_trade_outcome on close fills. The fill pipeline drives the full feedback loop with zero operator intervention. 36 new tests.
apex_predator_v0_1_50_fill_pipeline_auto.theme: v0.1.49 exposed the feedback hook; v0.1.50 closes the loop. Every entry signal in on_bar stashes an ActiveEntry with risk_usd + regime + strategy; every close fill in record_fill pops it, computes pnl_r, and feeds the retrospective manager. The self-evolving loop now runs end-to-end without the operator calling a single method.
apex_predator_v0_1_50_fill_pipeline_auto.operator_directive_quote: zero manual intervention. Entry fires -> risk captured. Fill closes -> pnl_r computed -> retrospective fires -> policy adapts. The bot is a closed loop from bar to decision to next sizing.

api_surface.Fill_risk_at_entry: float = 0.0. Optional per-fill risk-in-USD. Populated by the fill pipeline on close fills when the bot's in-memory ActiveEntry tracker was reset (post-reconnect). Backward-compatible; default 0 keeps every pre-v0.1.50 caller working unchanged.
api_surface.MnqBot_record_fill: (fill: Fill) -> RetrospectiveReport | None. Entry-point for the fill pipeline. Calls update_state unconditionally; on close (non-zero realized_pnl) pops the tracked ActiveEntry, computes pnl_r = realized_pnl / risk_usd, and auto-invokes record_trade_outcome with the entry's strategy + regime. Falls back to fill.risk_at_entry when no tracked entry exists.
api_surface.MnqBot_active_entries: read-only property returning a dict copy of currently tracked entries. For tests / dashboards.
api_surface.ActiveEntry: @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True). Fields: symbol, risk_usd, strategy, regime, opened_at_utc. Populated at entry-fire; consumed at close-fill.
api_surface.compute_risk_usd: (*, equity: float, risk_per_trade_pct: float) -> float. Centralized risk formula: equity * (risk_per_trade_pct / 100.0). Guards against non-positive inputs.
api_surface.is_entry_signal_type: (SignalType) -> bool. True for LONG / SHORT. Close and grid signals return False so they do not overwrite tracked entries.
api_surface.is_close_fill: (Fill) -> bool. True when abs(realized_pnl) > 1e-9. Defensive epsilon against simulator float noise.

design_notes.tracking_at_signal_fire_not_fill: The bot already knows the sizing math at on_bar; capturing risk there is simpler than parsing it from the Fill. Fill.risk_at_entry exists solely as a fallback for post-reconnect pipelines.
design_notes.per_symbol_key_single_position_invariant: Per-symbol dict assumes at most one open position per symbol (the fleet invariant). A second entry before a close overwrites the first -- acceptable for the pilot; logged in MnqBot.
design_notes.entry_fills_detected_by_zero_pnl: abs(realized_pnl) <= 1e-9 -> entry. Real brokers report exactly 0.0 on entry fills; epsilon is defensive against simulator float noise.
design_notes.no_base_bot_changes_beyond_fill: record_fill is per-subclass because retrospective state is per-bot. NQ / SOL / XRP adopt the same five-line method when promoted.
design_notes.failure_isolation_preserved: record_trade_outcome already wraps manager exceptions; record_fill defers to it. The fill pipeline never crashes the trading loop.
design_notes.stop_clears_active_entries: A reconnect reconciles live positions from the venue. The fill pipeline re-populates Fill.risk_at_entry on the next close, or the bot rebuilds entries on the next entry signal.

notable_cases: test_long_setup_signal_stashes_active_entry: an ORB-breakout LONG fires through on_bar and the bot's active_entries dict reflects the entry.

test_close_without_any_risk_is_noop: no tracked risk AND no fill.risk_at_entry means the auto-invoke silently skips rather than emitting a bogus pnl_r.
test_three_losses_via_record_fill_fire_streak: three back-to-back close fills with -50 realized / 50 tracked risk fire a LOSING_STREAK retrospective driven entirely by the fill pipeline.
test_record_fill_survives_manager_exception: a BoomManager raising from record_trade does not crash record_fill; the trading loop is protected.
test_both_bots_auto_feed_shared_manager: one shared manager receives auto-routed outcomes from both MnqBot and EthPerpBot via their respective record_fill calls.

design_notes.kwargs_forwarding_already_correct: All three derivative bots use **kwargs in __init__ and forward to parent. retrospective_manager, default_retrospective_strategy, and retrospective_sink flow through untouched. design_notes.on_bar_entry_tracking_inherited: Parent on_bar already calls _track_entry_from_signal on every LONG / SHORT signal. Derivative bots inherit on_bar (NqBot directly; SolPerpBot + XrpPerpBot via EthPerpBot), so entry tracking comes free.
design_notes.no_base_bot_changes: record_fill remains on MnqBot and EthPerpBot only. Subclasses inherit it. No duplication.

apex_predator_v0_1_55_safety_ops.theme: Closes the ops surface. Double-fills on reconnect, undrilled safety modules, sleeping operator, invisible model-tier burn, cold-operator VPS bring-up -- all addressed.
apex_predator_v0_1_55_safety_ops.operator_directive_quote: the bot must survive an Apex eval even when I'm asleep. No silent double-fills. No undrilled safety surface. No mobile-push blind spot. No mystery budget burn.

design_notes.conservative_reconciler: Local-only orders with venue missing are MARK_CANCELLED by default, never inferred alive. Conservative mode is the only mode on LIVE.
design_notes.chaos_matrix_single_source: All 16 safety surfaces enumerated. Adding a new safety module without its matrix row trips CI via --fail-under.
design_notes.mobile_push_severity_gated: Default min_severity=CRITICAL. Operator not woken for INFO / WARN chatter. Per-bus override.
design_notes.cost_ledger_sonnet_equiv_units: $ price per token can change without invalidating historical events. We store tokens + tier; unit conversion is a pure function of the current COST_RATIO table.
design_notes.host_runbook_stepwise: Every provisioning step is idempotent and re-runnable. Gates 4.1-4.5 must pass before LIVE flip. No surprise operator burn.

apex_predator_v0_1_56_chaos_drill_closur.bundle_name: CHAOS DRILL CLOSURE -- 12 new drills (one per [GAP] safety surface). Coverage 4/16 -> 16/16. Runner wired. Matrix locked at --fail-under 100.
apex_predator_v0_1_56_chaos_drill_closur.theme: The v0.1.55 matrix flagged 12 undrilled safety surfaces. v0.1.56 ships one rigorously-scoped drill per surface and pins every one at passed=True in pytest.
apex_predator_v0_1_56_chaos_drill_closur.operator_directive_quote: every safety surface must have a drill or it does not count as safe.

design_notes.one_drill_per_surface: Each safety surface gets its own module under scripts/chaos_drills/. One-file-per-drill keeps individual review cheap (<=150 lines) and isolates surface-specific stubs.
design_notes.shared_result_shape: _common.drill_result() emits the same 5-key dict every drill. Drift-proof output shape across 12 independent files.
design_notes.sandbox_hygiene: Drills never touch the network. Drills that need a clock inject a mutable _FakeClock wrapper rather than monkey-patching datetime.now.
design_notes.matrix_locked_at_100: tests/test_scripts_chaos_drill_matrix.py now asserts --fail-under 100 exits 0 (post-closure) and --fail-under 100.5 exits 1. Any new surface added without a drill trips CI.
design_notes.detector_reset_verified_by_state: pnl_drift drill checks detector.n == 0 and running_mean == 0.0 after the first alarm. This is stricter than observing the next update's shape, because PageHinkley's mean tracks the input stream.

apex_predator_v0_1_57_apex_eval_readines.bundle_name: APEX EVAL READINESS CLOSURE -- D1..D5 wired end-to-end in run_apex_live + MnqBot; bootstrap CI on real-MNQ OOS anchors expectancy in a confidence interval; NOISE verdict recorded.
apex_predator_v0_1_57_apex_eval_readines.theme: Every eval-readiness D-series surface flagged in the paper-run audit is now wired into the live tick-loop and covered by integration tests. Bootstrap CI on the real OOS R-distribution replaces the point-estimate headline with an honest CI that straddles zero at n=14.

apex_predator/bots/mnq/bot.py (SessionGate attach + on_bar EoD flatten path)

d_series_wiring.D1_session_gate: MnqBot.__init__ builds SessionGate from config; RouterAdapter receives the gate and rejects out-of-window entries with meta.gate_rejection.
d_series_wiring.D2_trailing_dd_tracker: ApexRuntime accepts trailing_dd_tracker kwarg. When attached, tick loop sums Tier-A equity, passes to tracker.update(), and uses the returned ApexEvalSnapshot in-place of build_apex_eval_snapshot. Legacy path preserved for rollout safety.
d_series_wiring.D3_consistency_guard: ApexRuntime accepts consistency_guard kwarg. Tick loop records today's tier-A session_realized_pnl via guard.record_eod; emits consistency_status alert + log entry exactly once on WARNING/VIOLATION enter.
d_series_wiring.D4_eod_flatten: MnqBot.on_bar queries adapter.should_flatten_eod each bar close; routes reason=apex_eod_flatten through kill-switch dispatch.

bootstrap_ci_mnq_oos.note: CI95 straddles zero; n=14 too thin to reject null. Point estimate +0.188R is informative but not validated edge. Future OOS trades via paper-run accumulation is the path to narrower CI.

design_choices.tracker_attach_is_opt_in: ApexRuntime constructs with tracker=None by default so existing callers keep the legacy path until the rollout flag flips.
design_choices.corrupt_state_fails_closed: Both trackers raise on any load-time integrity violation (baseline mismatch, unparseable date key). Silent reset would hide exactly the class of bug Apex is strict about.
design_choices.d3_alert_is_state_transition: Guard stores _last_consistency_status and only fires the alert when status *enters* WARNING/VIOLATION. Prevents spam while keeping steady-state visible in tick logs.
design_choices.bootstrap_verdict_recorded_with_point_es: Alpha-expansion's +0.188R point estimate is kept alongside the CI verdict so downstream readers cannot mistake it for a validated edge.

apex_predator_v0_1_58_red_team_closure.bundle_name: APEX EVAL READINESS HARDENING -- Red Team closure on v0.1.57 D-series wiring. Three BLOCKERs surfaced by risk-advocate adversarial review all closed in code + tests + docs.
apex_predator_v0_1_58_red_team_closure.theme: A safety module that is wired but whose wiring has a silent-failure gap is worse than no module: it produces false confidence. Every BLOCKER that could let the Apex eval bust while the runtime appears green is closed.

apex_predator/scripts/run_apex_live.py (B1 switch to session-day key; B2 live-mode tracker gate in ApexRuntime.__init__; B3 VIOLATION -> PAUSE_NEW_ENTRIES synthetic verdict + dispatch)

B1_session_day_bucketing.finding: utc_today_iso keyed today's consistency-guard entry by UTC calendar midnight. US equity-futures RTH + Globex overnight straddle UTC midnight so a single Apex trading day's PnL was split across two keys -- biasing the 30%-rule ratio downward and hiding real concentration risk.
B1_session_day_bucketing.fix: Added apex_trading_day_iso() using zoneinfo.ZoneInfo('America/Chicago') with 17:00 local rollover (DST-aware). Fallback to fixed 23:00-UTC rollover if zoneinfo unavailable. run_apex_live now calls this helper.

B2_live_mode_tracker_gate.finding: ApexRuntime defaulted to the bar-level build_apex_eval_snapshot proxy when no trailing_dd_tracker was supplied. The proxy lacks the Apex freeze rule (peak >= start + cap -> floor locks at start). A live runtime booted without the tracker would silently under-protect.
B2_live_mode_tracker_gate.fix: ApexRuntime.__init__ raises RuntimeError when cfg.live=True AND cfg.dry_run=False AND trailing_dd_tracker is None. Dry-run, paper-sim, and unit tests stay permissive.

B3_violation_enforces_pause.finding: On ConsistencyStatus.VIOLATION the guard emitted an alert + log but bot.state.is_paused stayed False. 'Notify the operator and keep trading' is not enforcement for an automated system.
B3_violation_enforces_pause.fix: Tick loop synthesizes KillVerdict(action=PAUSE_NEW_ENTRIES, severity=CRITICAL, scope='tier_a') on VIOLATION and feeds it through apply_verdict. Every tier-A bot flips is_paused=True. Existing positions are NOT flattened; only new entries blocked.

residual_risks_accepted.R1: logical equity vs broker MTM drift
residual_risks_accepted.R2: tick-interval latency vs Apex sub-second enforcement
residual_risks_accepted.R3: freeze-rule re-entrancy on tracker state deletion
residual_risks_accepted.R4: Apex session-day math across weekends/US holidays
residual_risks_accepted.note: All four documented in red_team_d2_d3_review.md sections R1..R4. Tracked for v0.2.x. None qualified as BLOCKER; D-series v0.1.58 baseline is strictly safer than v0.1.57 across every regime tested.

design_choices.dry_run_exempt_from_gate: B2 gate fires only when live=True AND dry_run=False. Dry-run callers keep the legacy proxy because the freeze-rule semantics do not matter when no real orders flow.
design_choices.violation_pauses_not_flattens: B3 fix flips is_paused but does not flatten. Existing positions can close on their own signals; only new entries are blocked. This is the minimum-viable enforcement that respects operator intent (maybe the eval is recoverable via a careful day) while stopping further concentration.
design_choices.fallback_rollover_is_23utc_not_22utc: When zoneinfo is missing, the fallback picks 23:00 UTC (CST winter rollover) rather than 22:00 UTC (CDT summer). Rationale: 23:00 UTC never falls mid-RTH session in either regime; 22:00 UTC in winter would split a live session. We accept being wrong by 1h in summer to guarantee correctness during RTH every day.
design_choices.utc_today_iso_kept_for_compat: utc_today_iso not deleted -- only deprecation-noted. External callers (tests in downstream repos, the_firm_complete) may depend on the UTC-midnight semantics for their own purposes; deletion would be a breaking change for an issue confined to Apex eval accounting.

R1_logical_vs_broker_mtm.finding: Tracker consumes sum(bot.state.equity) -- logical equity from the bot's own PnL book. Apex accounts for MTM at broker level (unrealized + realized + funding + fees). Prolonged disconnect silently over-states the cushion.
R1_logical_vs_broker_mtm.closure: BrokerEquityReconciler compares logical vs broker equity on every reconcile tick; classifies drift against configurable USD/pct tolerances; emits WARNING on broker_below_logical (dangerous), INFO on broker_above_logical. Does NOT pause/flatten/synthesize a KillVerdict. Source exceptions swallowed and treated as no_broker_data.
R1_logical_vs_broker_mtm.deferred_to_v0_2_x: Wiring each broker adapter's get_balance() / account-value endpoint to broker_equity_source. IBKR returns empty dict today; Tastytrade/Tradovate wiring is venue-specific. Once wired, the runtime can optionally swap logical equity for broker_equity - sum(open_pnl) as the tracker input.
R1_logical_vs_broker_mtm.tests: test_broker_equity_reconciler.py -- 21 tests: no-data path, within-tolerance, broker_below_logical, broker_above_logical, USD/pct tolerance boundaries, zero logical equity, source-raising treated as no_data, stats counters, result-shape contract.

R2_tick_interval_latency.finding: Runtime polled on a 5-second tick. A fast retrace during that window could cross the floor before the next update. Apex enforcement is likely sub-second.
R2_tick_interval_latency.closure: validate_apex_tick_cadence enforces tick_interval_s * max_usd_move_per_sec * safety_factor <= cushion_usd (defaults $300/sec x 2.0). RuntimeConfig.tick_interval_s default reduced 5.0 -> 1.0. Validator runs in load_runtime_config and raises ApexTickCadenceError in live mode on violation.

R3_freeze_rule_reentrancy.finding: Tracker freezes at peak >= start + cap. If the state file is accidentally deleted or the operator re-inits with a larger cap, the freeze is lost and the floor resumes trailing.
R3_freeze_rule_reentrancy.closure: TrailingDDAuditLog append-only JSONL with fsync per append. Lifecycle events (init/load/freeze/breach/reset) recorded immutably. reset() now requires operator + acknowledge_destruction or raises ResetNotAcknowledgedError. Audit log survives state file deletion -- forensic review can always detect a silent re-init.
R3_freeze_rule_reentrancy.tests: 6 new classes in test_trailing_dd_tracker.py: TestAuditLogInitAndLoad, TestAuditLogFreezeAndBreach, TestAuditLogSequenceMonotonicity, TestResetAcknowledgment, TestAuditLogSurvivesStateDeletion, TestTrailingDDAuditLogUnit.

R4_session_day_weekends_holidays.finding: apex_trading_day_iso keyed Saturday-morning timestamps to 'Saturday' which Apex ignores, creating phantom zero-PnL buckets in the 30%-rule denominator.
R4_session_day_weekends_holidays.closure: core/events_calendar.py -- CME Globex session calendar with dateutil.easter-driven Good Friday + fixed-date closures. apex_trading_day_iso now routes through is_trading_day / next_trading_day to roll weekend/holiday timestamps forward to the next regular trading day.
R4_session_day_weekends_holidays.tests: test_core_events_calendar.py (full CME calendar) + test_consistency_guard.py extensions for each closure type.

design_choices.r1_observation_not_enforcement: R1 reconciler is pure observation -- it logs drift but does NOT pause/flatten/synthesize KillVerdict. Rationale: until the broker adapter is wired, every tick would classify as no_broker_data; flipping that to an actionable signal requires a venue integration decision that is v0.2.x scope. Shipping the observation layer now means v0.2.x is a wiring diff, not a wiring + policy diff.
design_choices.r2_live_only_validator: validate_apex_tick_cadence is a no-op in paper/dry-run. Rationale: the whole point of the inequality is real-dollar risk per tick; paper mode has no real dollars, and dry-run is a test harness. A validator that blocks dev iteration would cause devs to disable it.
design_choices.r2_default_bound_300usd_per_sec: max_usd_move_per_sec default is $300 -- bounds a ~6-handle move on 2 MNQ contracts ($5/handle/contract = $10 per handle per contract = $60 for 6 handles per contract, ~$120 on 2 contracts) with headroom for flash-spike behaviour. Safety factor 2.0 bounds worst-case miss-by-one-tick. For tick=1.0s, cushion must be >= $600 in live mode.
design_choices.r3_audit_log_colocation: Audit log defaults to <state_path>.audit.jsonl -- same directory so operators find it naturally. Tradeoff: an attacker who can delete state can also delete the audit log. Mitigation: the audit log path is constructor-configurable, so production can point it at a write-once or replicated volume.
design_choices.r3_reset_ack_not_prompt: reset() requires a programmatic acknowledge_destruction=True, not an interactive prompt. Rationale: the tracker is called from runtime code and scripts, both headless. An interactive prompt would crash non-tty contexts. The operator triggers reset via a CLI wrapper that collects the ack at the CLI layer.
design_choices.r4_calendar_uses_dateutil: Good Friday is Easter-adjacent; Easter date requires Computus (Gauss / Meeus algorithm). dateutil.easter is vendored-quality and already a project dependency. Hand-rolling Computus is not worth it.

apex_predator_v0_1_60_preflight_hardenin.bundle_name: APEX EVAL PREFLIGHT-HARDENING -- surface R2 tick-cadence and R3 audit-log fsync invariants at preflight so the operator validates the config BEFORE live startup instead of discovering the mis-config at the first tick.
apex_predator_v0_1_60_preflight_hardenin.theme: v0.1.59 closed R2/R3 in runtime code. Both are correct-by-construction but both fail LATE -- undersized cushion crashes run_apex_live at startup, read-only state volume crashes TrailingDDAuditLog at the first freeze event. scripts.preflight is the designated 'can we go live NOW' gate; it did not surface these two invariants. v0.1.60 wires them in so the operator sees red BEFORE flipping the switch.

design_choices.live_mode_validator_at_preflight: check_tick_cadence calls the validator with live=True unconditionally. Rationale: preflight IS the 'about to go live' gate. Running it in paper mode would mask the real question -- 'would this config pass the live-mode invariant'. Operators doing a pure paper session don't run preflight.
design_choices.default_cushion_fallback_500: If tier_a.apex_eval_preemptive.cushion_usd is missing/empty, fall back to 500.0 (the pre-v0.1.59 config value). This means an empty block fails the check with the same error the live runtime would produce -- no silent fallback to a passing value.
design_choices.audit_dir_uses_tracker_default: check_audit_log_readiness probes <repo>/state, the tracker's default. Rationale: the tracker constructor picks the default; reproducing it here keeps the two in lockstep without introducing a shared-constants protocol. If a future release changes the default, the constant here (and its matching test monkeypatch) move in one diff.
design_choices.tempfile_cleanup_in_finally: The happy path unlinks the tempfile before return; the finally block re-attempts with missing_ok=True so a partial fsync failure doesn't leave litter in state/. Belt and braces -- if the first unlink succeeded, the second is a no-op.
design_choices.run_async_test_helper_refactor: _stub_all_checks_green centralises the green-stub setup that used to be duplicated across 4 test functions. Adding check #8 is now a one-line diff in the helper instead of an N-file sweep.

scope_exclusions.R1_broker_equity_drift: Scaffolded in v0.1.59 but not surfaced at preflight. Wiring the reconciler here is blocked on broker adapter get_balance implementations (venue-specific, v0.2.x scope). When wired, a 5th closure-surface row lands here.
scope_exclusions.R4_session_day_calendar: In-process invariant -- nothing external to probe at preflight time. The calendar is tested offline and fails closed (unknown dates are non-trading days), so there is no live-boot risk.

preflight_output_shape.ordering_rationale: tick_cadence and audit_log placed between firm_verdict and telegram so the ordering goes 'can we connect -> is the market open -> are we risk-cleared -> is the config sane -> is the IO path sane -> can we alert'.
