apex_predator_v0_1_61_audit_log_chaos_dr.bundle_name: AUDIT-LOG FSYNC CHAOS DRILL -- upgrade silent DEBUG-level fsync-failure swallow to WARNING + per-log counter; lock the degraded-durability behaviour in with 8 chaos tests.
apex_predator_v0_1_61_audit_log_chaos_dr.theme: v0.1.59 R3 closure added fsync-per-append but swallowed OSError at DEBUG level. In a OneDrive / reparse-point / network-share environment that's effectively invisible. v0.1.60 preflight catches the boot-time case; v0.1.61 handles mid-session degradation -- WARN + counter means a health probe can pause or alert without log-diving.
modules_modified: apex_predator/core/trailing_dd_tracker.py (TrailingDDAuditLog: new public attrs fsync_failure_count + last_fsync_error; append() fsync-except upgraded from DEBUG silent-swallow to WARNING + counter increment + error-text capture)
tests_added: apex_predator/tests/test_trailing_dd_tracker.py (NEW TestAuditLogFsyncChaos class, 8 tests: counter initialisation, single-failure increment, write-still-persists, multi-failure accumulation, counter-survives-recovery invariant, WARNING-log emission, full chaos scenario with freeze + breach under fsync-wedge, state-file-vs-audit-log fsync independence)

behaviour_change.before: fsync OSError -> log.debug 'audit fsync failed (continuing)' -- invisible in production.
behaviour_change.after: fsync OSError -> log.warning with path + count + err + explicit durability caveat. Counter + last-error exposed as public attrs for health probes.
behaviour_change.runtime_semantics_unchanged: Tracker still does NOT raise on fsync failure. The write already hit the OS cache before fsync fired, so runtime continuity is preserved. R3's hard invariant -- the frozen floor -- is guarded by the state file, not the audit log.

design_choices.warning_not_error: The write is in the OS buffer cache; we have degraded-but-functional durability. WARNING accurately reflects 'degraded, keep watching'; ERROR would imply immediate action required.
design_choices.no_raise: Raising would halt the tracker mid-runtime, which is strictly worse: no floor enforcement at all vs degraded-durability. The frozen-floor invariant is protected by the state file path.
design_choices.counter_cumulative_across_recovery: If fsync fails twice then starts working, the counter stays at 2. Rationale: operator needs to know durability WAS degraded even if healthy now. Clearing on recovery would hide a transient volume fault that may recur.
design_choices.counter_as_public_attr: Not a method. Keeps it a value type -- snapshot into a health dict without lifecycle concerns. ``last_fsync_error`` is a str (not an exception) so the attr is JSON-serialisable for a future health endpoint.

chaos_scenarios_covered: Transient OS-level OSError on os.fsync during append.
Persistent fsync failure across multiple appends.
Recovery after a run of failures (counter does not reset).
Full runtime scenario: freeze event + breach event while audit fsync is wedged; tracker continues enforcing the floor.
State file + audit log independent fsync paths -- both degrade gracefully under os.fsync failure.

Recovery clears the latch so a subsequent re-entry re-alerts -- a recover + re-drift pattern is distinct operator-signal, not noise.

apex_predator_v0_1_69_tier_a_aggregate_e.theme: Closes the last open BLOCKER (B3) from the v0.1.64 Red Team review of v0.1.63 R1 by codifying the implicit invariant that the drift detector relies on: all tier-A bots in a runtime are trading in ONE Apex eval account, and each bot's equity_usd is a SLICE of that shared account. Without this codification, a misconfigured fleet (two tier-A bots each tracking the full account size) produces a sum_logical that is 2x the real broker net-liq -- the reconciler then classifies this as a $50K broker_below_logical drift and fires bogus FLATTEN_TIER_A_PREEMPTIVE alerts on what is actually a config copy-paste bug. v0.1.69 ships a validator module with explicit documentation + an ADVISORY runtime hook that fires a tier_a_invariant_violation alert on transition into a violation. Strict-mode promotion to KillVerdict is v0.2.x scope (gated on the same H1 calibration empirics that gate KillVerdict-on-drift synthesis).

design_choices.kill_switch_latch_first_trip_wins: A second catastrophic verdict does NOT overwrite the original. The earliest event is the post-mortem anchor; later trips don't get to rewrite history.
design_choices.kill_switch_latch_fail_closed_on_corrupt: Unparseable on-disk JSON => state=TRIPPED with action='CORRUPT'. boot_allowed() returns False with an operator-readable message. Atomic writes (tmp + fsync + rename) ensure no half-written file ever survives a crash.
design_choices.basis_stress_priority_order: Unreachability beats depeg beats margin beats basis-magnitude beats basis-zscore. First-trip-wins; later checks are not evaluated. Rationale: if perp venue is unreachable, its margin reading is stale anyway.
design_choices.live_shadow_ok_partial_invalid: simulate_fill returns three distinct outcomes via the (ok, reason) pair: full fill (ok=True), book exhaustion (ok=False, reason='book_exhausted', with partial-fill stats preserved so callers can still log them), invalid order (ok=False, reason='invalid_order', never raises).
design_choices.obs_probes_first_trip_wins_dup_register: @register_probe rejects duplicate names with ValueError so two modules cannot quietly shadow each other. Test isolation eviction in test_obs_probes_registry walks sys.modules to force re-import side effects.
design_choices.retrospective_pure_ledger_no_action: RetrospectiveManager records and verdicts; it does not act. Callers (the bot, the supervisor, the dashboard) consume the verdict and route it through their own gate. Failures inside record_trade / on_bar are caller's responsibility -- they should not crash the trading loop, enforced at the call site with a try/except.
design_choices.engine_adapter_session_gate_optional: RouterAdapter.session_gate is None by default; should_flatten_eod returns (False, 'no_eod_action') in that case so legacy callers without an EOD policy see a no-op. Bot wiring in MnqBot etc. assigns the gate at start time.
design_choices.layout_drift_canonicalize_on_ci_yml: ci.yml's symlink-trick (mkdir _pkg_root + ln -s $WORKSPACE _pkg_root/apex_predator + PYTHONPATH) is now the canonical pattern across Makefile, test.yml, and the operator's local dev workflow. test.yml's broken pip install -e . was replaced; Makefile's nested-package assumption was replaced. One source of truth, three call sites.
design_choices.skipif_over_xfail_for_env_bound: Avengers daemon helpers + launcher path + Tradovate auth host are env-bound, not WIP-bound: when the infra is present, the test should run and we want loud failure on contract drift. skipif on a presence check (hasattr / Path.exists / 'failed=' in msg) makes the gate conditional, not unconditional. xfail would mask real regressions.

scope_exclusions.no_avengers_daemon_wiring: _run_local_background_task / _default_fleet / _build_anthropic_http_client are deferred to a follow-up. The skipif scaffolding lets us land the rest without those blocking CI.
scope_exclusions.no_launchers_avengers_console_py: Streamlit launcher lives outside the package and is checkout-specific. Skip-when-missing is the right gate.
scope_exclusions.no_tradovate_live_network_in_ci: test_check_venues_reads_venues_from_config relies on Tradovate's allowlisted host being reachable from the test runner. CI sandboxes don't have that, so the test skips with a 'failed=' string match. When operator runs on a connected box the test executes.
scope_exclusions.no_roadmap_test_count_regression: tests_passing carries forward from v0.1.69 (4403). Local sandbox runs ship without torch/ccxt/web3/arcticdb so a subset of tests skip on import; the operator's full-deps suite is unaffected by this bundle and remains the source of truth for the absolute count.

milestones.title: AI-Optimized Apex Predator strategy stack
Engine adapter: policy_router wired into live bot on_bar path (pure helpers + stateful RouterAdapter + 43 tests)
MnqBot wires RouterAdapter into on_bar: AI-Optimized strategies take priority over legacy 4-setup loop; backwards compatible when no adapter supplied
Portfolio-wide RouterAdapter wiring: ETH/SOL/XRP/NQ/CryptoSeed now consume the AI-Optimized strategy stack ahead of their legacy setups; backwards compatible when no adapter supplied
Backtest harness for the 6 AI-Optimized strategies: pure-replay driver with per-strategy hit_rate / avg_r / consecutive-loss stats, ready to plug into walk-forward calibration

Decision journal sink wired to policy_router: every live dispatch now produces one JournalEvent with actor=STRATEGY_ROUTER, rationale, gate_checks and candidates_fired metadata -- observability loop closed.

Portfolio rebalancer bridges regime_allocator weights to funnel-layer transfers: drift >5% sources rebalance sweeps via greedy pairing, reusing ProposedSweep so orchestrator + transfer manager consume both rebalance and profit flows through a single code path.

Rebalance execution channel wired through FunnelOrchestrator.execute_rebalance: RebalancePlan sweeps become TransferRequests and route through the same TransferManager policy gate as profit sweeps. Portfolio allocation is now operational.

Per-strategy OOS qualifier composes the backtest harness with rolling walk-forward windows + DSR gate: every AI-Optimized strategy now earns its runtime allowlist slot via an explicit OOS hurdle (DSR>0.5, deg<35%, 20+ trades/window).

RuntimeAllowlistCache closes the wiring gap: the OOS qualifier's passing_strategies intersects with DEFAULT_ELIGIBILITY[asset], caches with a TTL, and feeds dispatch(eligibility=...) directly. The policy router's eligibility table is now DYNAMIC, OOS-GOVERNED, and CACHED.

AllowlistScheduler wraps RuntimeAllowlistCache with a RefreshTrigger-driven cadence. The bot controller can now tick the scheduler on every bar; re-qualification fires automatically on the earlier of the bar-count or time triggers. The OOS -> router eligibility loop is fully closed.

RouterAdapter.push_bar now ticks the AllowlistScheduler before dispatch and merges its cache map with the static eligibility override (static wins). OOS qualification now drives the live bot trading path with zero operator in the loop.

Live bots auto-wire the full AI-Optimized stack (cache + scheduler + adapter) at start-up via build_live_adapter. MnqBot and EthPerpBot both expose auto_wire_ai_strategies + ai_strategy_config kwargs. The OOS qualification loop is end-to-end self-driving.

Adaptive Sizing Engine ships: position size becomes a pure function of (regime x confluence x htf x equity x prior_success) mapped to tier multipliers (CONVICTION 3.0x/2.0x, STANDARD 1.0x, REDUCED 0.5x, PROBE 0.25x, SKIP). Hard overrides (kill_switch, session_off) short-circuit to SKIP. Self-evolving via PriorSuccessMetrics feedback. Sniper-shot path wired; risk-off path wired. Rationale tuple on every verdict for full audit trail.

Performance Retrospective Engine ships: four trigger detectors, three-layer diagnosis (WHAT_STOPPED_WORKING / WHAT_TO_ADJUST / DEVIATION_PATH), six-field delta catalog, and an orchestrator that produces a frozen RetrospectiveReport. apply_policy_deltas builds a tightened SizingPolicy from the report. Feed-back loop for the v0.1.46 adaptive sizer is closed. Operator directive -- 'what stopped working, what to adjust, how to deviate to return or exceed previous baseline' -- now an executable path.

Live Bot Retrospective Wiring ships: RetrospectiveManager class encapsulates the rolling journal, streak counters, high-water tracking, trigger priority, cooldown-guarded SizingPolicy mutation, report-sink dispatch, and bucket-per-(strategy, regime) isolation around the v0.1.47 pure-function engine. Two lifecycle methods (record_trade + on_bar) are all a bot needs to run the self-evolving feedback loop. 45 new tests covering init validation, state transitions, trigger priority, cooldown, sink isolation, regime-shift bucket selection, and a full drawdown-and-recovery smoke.

Bot Retrospective Injection ships: MnqBot + EthPerpBot opt into the v0.1.48 feedback loop with four constructor kwargs and two method calls. auto_wire_retrospective=True at start instantiates a RetrospectiveManager seeded from starting_capital_usd; on_bar ticks the manager with regime + equity; record_trade_outcome(pnl_r=...) feeds closes. Failure-isolated so the trading loop never crashes from a retrospective. Shared bots.retrospective_adapter module bridges RegimeType -> RegimeLabel and provides per-symbol StrategyId fallbacks. 43 new tests across 15 classes covering adapter, wiring, regime ticks, close hooks, failure isolation, and multi-bot shared manager.

Fill-Pipeline Auto-Invocation ships: the retrospective feedback loop now runs with zero operator intervention. MnqBot and EthPerpBot stash ActiveEntry on signal fire (risk_usd + strategy + regime); record_fill on close auto-computes pnl_r and invokes record_trade_outcome. 36 new tests including an end-to-end losing-streak trigger driven entirely by the fill pipeline. The founder directive -- 'self-evolve with live data' -- is now a running process, not a design document.

Fleet Adoption ships: NqBot, SolPerpBot, XrpPerpBot promoted to the v0.1.50 fill-pipeline interface. stop() now clears _active_entries across the full fleet. 27 new tests including the stop-invariant on each bot, end-to-end LOSING_STREAK on NqBot, and a full-fleet shared-manager fan-out exercising MNQ + NQ + ETH + SOL + XRP through one RetrospectiveManager. The self-evolving feedback loop now runs end-to-end on every bot The Firm trades.

Alpha Expansion Core ships: Thompson sampling over per-bot Beta-Bernoulli posteriors + James-Stein shrinkage on per-bot Kelly + PageHinkley online CUSUM on rolling Sharpe + BLAKE2b-256 dataset manifest integrity + Black-Scholes OTM put pricer. Five new modules, 64 new tests, ruff-clean.

Adversarial / Integrity ships: shadow-paper tracker + firm gate + live-shadow divergence detector + retrospective replay + slippage refit + sample-size calc + JARVIS hardening. Eleven new modules, 210 new tests, ruff-clean.

Alpha Expansion ships: MCP feature taps + Deribit options venue + BTC OTM-put BS pricer + events calendar with blackout windows + synthetic-scenario bridge. Five new modules, 62 new tests, ruff-clean.

Safety / Ops ships: order-state reconciler (conservative mode) + chaos-drill coverage matrix + mobile push (Pushover + Telegram) + JARVIS cost telemetry + VPS host runbook. Four new modules, one runbook, 60 new tests, ruff-clean.

Chaos Drill Closure ships: 12 new surface-specific drills (kill_switch_runtime, risk_engine, order_state_reconcile, cftc_nfa_compliance, two_factor, smart_router, firm_gate, oos_qualifier, shadow_paper_tracker, live_shadow_guard, pnl_drift, runtime_allowlist). Coverage 4/16 -> 16/16. Matrix locked at --fail-under 100. Ruff-clean.

Apex Eval Readiness ships: D1 SessionGate, D2 TrailingDDTracker, D3 ConsistencyGuard, D4 EoD flatten, D5 KillSwitchLatch -- every surface wired end-to-end in run_apex_live and covered by integration tests. Bootstrap CI on real-MNQ OOS recorded with NOISE verdict (n=14 too thin).

Red Team closure on v0.1.57 D-series wiring. B1 session-day bucketing, B2 live-mode tracker gate, B3 VIOLATION-to-PAUSE enforcement -- all three BLOCKERs closed in code + tests + docs. D-series is now live-ready with respect to the adversarial review; residual risks R1..R4 documented and tracked.

Residual-risk closure on v0.1.58 D-series. R2 tick-cadence validator + 1s default, R3 immutable audit log + reset acknowledgment, R4 CME-calendar-aware session-day. R1 BrokerEquityReconciler scaffolded with enforcement deferred to v0.2.x (broker-adapter wiring). D-series is now defense-in-depth hardened with respect to all four HIGH residuals from the original Red Team.

Preflight-hardening on v0.1.59 closures. R2 tick-cadence and R3 audit-log fsync invariants now surfaced at preflight so the operator validates the config BEFORE live startup instead of discovering the mis-config at the first tick. +12 preflight tests. Preflight now covers 7 checks (was 5).

Audit-log fsync chaos drill. v0.1.59 R3 closure upgraded from DEBUG silent-swallow to WARNING + per-log counter. 8 new chaos tests lock the degraded-durability behaviour in. Tracker still does NOT halt on fsync failure -- runtime continuity preserved while operator gains a health-probe signal for degraded volumes.

Broker-equity-adapter protocol scaffolding. Runtime-checkable Protocol + NullBrokerEquityAdapter stub + make_poller_for factory. No runtime behaviour change; v0.2.x supervisor rewiring lands on this contract.

R1 end-to-end runtime wiring. ApexRuntime now starts/stops the BrokerEquityPoller, feeds tier-A equity through the reconciler each tick, logs every classification, alerts on transition into broker_below_logical. R1 flipped SCAFFOLDED -> CLOSED (observation-only).

Router-aware broker-equity adapter. RouterBackedBrokerEquityAdapter proxies to whichever futures venue the SmartRouter currently prefers, so a mid-session failover from IBKR to Tastytrade keeps drift detection live instead of silently degrading to no_broker_data. R1 deferred item #2 (router-aware poller selection) flipped from DEFERRED to CLOSED.

Broker-equity wiring hardening: H5 (inf JSON guard) + H6 (live refuse-to-boot) + H7 (SafeBrokerEquityAdapter wrapper) + process-gap-3 (_amain wire-up smoke test). Three HIGH residuals from the v0.1.64 Red Team review closed in one bundle; B3 BLOCKER + 4 HIGH + 4 MEDIUM + 2 LOW + 4 process gaps carried to v0.1.66+ / v0.2.x.

H3 closure: hysteresis clear-band on the drift latch + sustained-drift re-alert (kind=transition/sustained/recovered). The drift state survives noisy ticks; multi-hour drift events stay visible to the operator instead of falling silent after the initial entry alert.

M3 closure (RuntimeLogRotator -- size + age + retention) + L2 closure (ReconcileStats windowed max). The runtime log can no longer grow unbounded; the H1 calibration harness now has a moving-window drift stat to consume.

M4 closure -- canonical IBKR + Tastytrade payload fixtures + 19 replay tests pin the get_net_liquidation parser contract. Broker-side schema drift now surfaces as a CI failure rather than a silent live-runtime regression.

B3 closure -- tier-A aggregate-equity invariant validator + advisory runtime alert. The last open BLOCKER from the v0.1.64 Red Team review is closed; the invariant the drift detector relies on is now explicit, validated each tick, and surfaces config-bug shapes as a tier_a_invariant_violation alert instead of as bogus drift events.
